title: Week 8 Case Stud1
body:
Week 8 Case Study Agentic AI for Business Analytics - RetailPulse Inc.

Background RetailPulse Inc. is a mid-sized Australian retailer headquartered in Sydney, operating 45 physical stores across New South Wales and Victoria. The company sells electronics, home goods, seasonal products, and apparel through both physical stores and an online platform, generating approximately $50 million in annual revenue.

RetailPulse’s current forecasting process is manual and time-consuming. A team of four business analysts pulls data from three separate systems (point-of-sale, inventory management, and online sales platform), consolidates it in Excel spreadsheets, and produces demand forecasts using basic statistical methods. This process takes approximately two weeks per forecasting cycle.

The Business Problem The manual forecasting process creates several critical challenges: • Forecasts are frequently outdated by delivery time • Data consolidation introduces inconsistencies • Stockouts cost approximately $2 million annually in lost revenue • Overstocking ties up approximately $1.5 million in excess inventory

The Proposed Solution Build a two-agent agentic AI system using open-source LLMs (Ollama + LangChain) that collaboratively analyses sales data and generates prompt-driven managerial insights: • Data Analysis Agent (LLM 1 - llama3.1): Implements the Tool Use design pattern. This agent has access to four specialised tools: (1) load_and_profile_data - loads and cleans the CSV sales data, (2) engineer_features_and_train_models - creates 17 ML features and trains three forecasting models (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Linear Regression), (3) compute_business_metrics - calculates promotion effectiveness, seasonal patterns, and store performance. (4) create_visualisations - generates analytical charts. The LLM autonomously decides which tools to call and in what order. • Business Insights Agent (LLM 2 - llama3.1): Implements the Planning design pattern. This agent takes the analysis results from Agent 1 and a user prompt as input. It generates tailored managerial insights based on the user’s specified role and questions. Different prompts produce different insights - a Store Manager receives operational recommendations, a Marketing Director receives campaign strategy, and a CFO receives financial impact analysis.  Both agents use llama3.1, an open-source LLM by Meta, running locally via Ollama. The agent framework is built with LangChain, following the same pattern as the email-sending agent from Hands-On Activity 1.

Hands-On Instructions Step 1: Set Up Your Environment Install the required Python packages:

pip install pandas numpy scikit-learn matplotlib

Ensure Ollama is installed and running with a model:

ollama pull llama3.1

Step 2: Load and Explore the Data Open the file RetailPulse_Sales_Data.csv. This dataset contains daily sales records across 5 stores and 4 product categories for the year 2024. Open the Jupyter notebook RetailPulse_MultiAgent_Workflow.ipynb. Run the cells in order. The Data Analysis Agent will automatically load data, train models, and compute metrics.

# Load and explore data

Examine the output: How many rows? What are the columns? Are there missing values?

Step 3: Feature Engineering The agentic workflow creates time-based features that help the ML model detect patterns: • month, day_of_week, is_weekend, quarter: Capture seasonal and weekly patterns • sales_lag_7: Sales from 7 days ago (weekly pattern) • sales_rolling_30: 30-day rolling average (trend) • promotion, temperature: External factors affecting demand

Step 4: Try Different Prompts for Different Insights After the Data Analysis Agent completes, try entering different prompts to the Business Insights Agent. For example: 1. As a Store Manager: Ask about staffing, inventory planning, and operational efficiency 2. As a Marketing Director: Ask about promotion ROI, campaign timing, and seasonal strategy 3. As a CFO: Ask about cost savings, revenue impact, and store performance 4. As a Customer: Ask about best shopping times and deals Observe how the same data produces different actionable insights depending on your role and questions.

Discussion Questions  Q1: Business Value Assessment How would the proposed agentic AI workflow reduce RetailPulse’s forecasting cycle from two weeks to under one hour? What specific steps in the current manual process are eliminated or automated? Estimate the potential cost savings.

Notes

Q2: Trust and Adoption Strategy If you were the Chief Operating Officer at RetailPulse, would you trust an AI agent to make inventory decisions for a $50M revenue business? What human oversight mechanisms would you recommend?

Notes

Q3: Prompt Engineering and Role-Based Insights How did the insights change when you switched from a Store Manager prompt to a Marketing Director prompt to a CFO prompt? What does this tell us about the value of prompt engineering in agentic AI? Design a prompt for a new role (e.g., Supply Chain Manager or Customer Experience Director) and describe what insights you would expect.

Notes

References • Andrew Ng, Agentic AI Course, DeepLearning.AI (2024–2025) • Russell, S. J., & Norvig, P. (2020). Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach (4th ed.). Pearson. • LangChain Documentation. python.langchain.com/docs • Ollama. ollama.com
